articles.summary: Spreadsheets for timelines. Another app for approvals. Email threads nobody can find. Sound familiar? Asana [4] turns the chaos of 12 into the simplicity of 1.

articles.title: APPLE TURNS TO GOOGLE TO POWER AI UPGRADE FOR SIRI
articles.summary: Apple is upgrading Siri and other services by relying on Google Gemini AI. The multi-year deal reflects that its internal AI could not match Google in capability or scale in the short term, even though it plans to keep Apple Intelligence running on its devices and private cloud systems. Analysts expect consumers to welcome the move as AI features spread across smartphones, even if AI is not yet a primary driver of iPhone purchases. The partnership may raise regulatory scrutiny since past Google deals on Apple devices were worth billions of dollars, including more than $26bn paid in 2021 to secure default search placement.

articles.title: BIFURCATION OF THE US ECONOMY HITS THE SHOPPING MALL
articles.summary: US shopping malls show a widening split between a small group of strong performers and a large group in decline as the economy bifurcates. The top 100 of roughly 900 malls hold about 50% of total asset value, while the bottom 350 hold just 10%, and C and D malls saw occupancy fall by more than 26 percentage points from 2016 to 2019. Pressure is rising as lower-income consumers face higher bills, and 34% of consumers plan to spend less this year, with 43% citing tariffs. Brands and retailers must either move upmarket to target the top 10% that drives 50% of US spending or compete harder on value, which requires strong brand equity to support pricing and promotions.

articles.title: ZUMBA'S REVENUE GROWTH FROM APP2WEB FLOWS
articles.summary: Zumba increased lifetime value by over 17% by shifting high-intent users from in-app purchases to app2web checkout, accepting lower initial conversion in exchange for higher retention, higher trial-to-paid rates, and elimination of app store fees. Conversion friction was reduced by mirroring native purchase flows, pre-selecting plans in-app, defaulting to Apple Pay or Google Pay, and auto-redirecting users back to the app post-purchase.

articles.title: HOW TO PROVE IMPACT FROM LIFECYCLE MARKETING
articles.summary: Impact shows in behavior change. Users adopt features, reach value faster, ask questions, or re-engage before churn. Focus on one key behavior, compare cohorts instead of campaigns, and track metrics from immediate actions to long-term outcomes. A simple spreadsheet can reveal real change. Show what happens after a click or nudge to demonstrate value. Lifecycle marketing works by changing how customers engage and find value, not by linking every action directly to revenue.

articles.title: HOW SIMPLE SEMANTICS INCREASED OUR AI CITATIONS BY 642%
articles.summary: HubSpot increased page citations by AI tools by rewriting key content into semantic triples (sentences structured as subject-predicate-object). Pages were restructured so core concepts appeared early and in answer-first phrasing, helping AI models reliably associate HubSpot with categories like marketing automation. This change was implemented alongside existing SEO fundamentals such as schema and backlinks.

articles.title: 🥘 8 RECIPES FOR REPURPOSING VIDEOS INTO A CONTENT BUFFET
articles.summary: Fresh content is always in short supply. Are you making the most of what's already in your pantry? Wistia has assembled the Guide to Repurposing [10] to help you turn every creative into a month's worth of content.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY AT WORK: A GUIDE TO GETTING MORE DONE
articles.summary: This guide shows how to use Perplexity as a unified workspace to scale your work and move faster. It outlines prompts for research, email triage, meeting prep, document lookup, and multi-step browser actions. It also includes tips like using @tab for context and breaking complex tasks into steps. It shares tactics for reducing switching by letting Comet and Email Assistant summarize information, manage inboxes, group tabs, schedule meetings, and automate recurring routines with shortcuts and tasks.

articles.title: ABOVE THE FOLD (EVENT)
articles.summary: This 2-day in-person event in Fort Lauderdale gives B2B SaaS marketers practical website tactics through small group workshops. Facilitators include Anthony Pierri, Tas Bober, Jess Cook, Rob Kaminski, and more. Sessions cover landing pages, content strategy, positioning, pricing pages, and demos. Attendees can choose a $660 ticket that includes three pitch room sessions or a $990 ticket with workshop access only.

articles.title: IS IT TIME TO KILL ALL THE MASCOTS?
articles.summary: Creative teams should challenge default mascot requests and evaluate whether other brand assets can better communicate value. Strategic alternatives such as distinctive color systems and direct language can stand out more effectively than anthropomorphic characters. Mascots should be a deliberate choice tied to long-term brand strategy, not a reflexive response to category trends or competitors.

articles.title: NETFLIX WANTED TO REINVENT LIVE TV. IT HASN'T BEEN EASY
articles.summary: Netflix is expanding into live programming to challenge traditional TV, but reliable live streaming has proved harder than expected. The company has aired more than 200 events, yet big moments like the Jake Paul–Mike Tyson fight revealed limits in delivering millions of unicast streams at once. Netflix has since upgraded routing, tested fixes through smaller livestreams, and built a live operations center. It says performance is improving as it prepares for a broader global live push in 2026.

articles.title: PINTEREST SAYS GEN Z USERS ARE FINDING THEMSELVES IN PINS
articles.summary: Pinterest is positioning visual search and customization as higher-intent pathways to purchase than AI chatbots or text-heavy platforms.

articles.title: WHAT TO DO IF YOUR RESEARCH IS FALLING FLAT
articles.summary: Effective insights are paired with an interpretation of impact and a recommended path forward.

articles.title: AMAZON SUPERSIZES ITS WALMART RIVALRY WITH NEW BIG-BOX RETAIL CONCEPT
articles.summary: Amazon's superstore concept builds on recent Whole Foods tests that merge in-store browsing with automated fulfillment and aims to capture more spending that still happens in physical stores.
